states.name: Alabama
hiddenGems: Dismals Canyon — a privately owned natural wonder with bioluminescent creatures called Dismalites
Ave Maria Grotto in Cullman — 125 miniature reproductions of famous religious buildings
Coon Dog Cemetery in Cherokee — the only cemetery in the world dedicated to coon dogs
Noccalula Falls Park in Gadsford — a stunning 90-foot waterfall with a pioneer village
mustSeeSpots: Gulf Shores and Orange Beach — Alabama's beautiful Gulf Coast beaches
U.S. Space & Rocket Center in Huntsville — home of Space Camp
Civil Rights District in Birmingham — 16th Street Baptist Church and Kelly Ingram Park
Montgomery Civil Rights Trail — Rosa Parks Museum, Dexter Avenue King Memorial Church
insiderTips: Try white BBQ sauce — a tangy mayo-based sauce unique to northern Alabama
Visit the Unclaimed Baggage Center in Scottsboro to shop lost airline luggage items
Drive the Natchez Trace Parkway through the northwestern corner for gorgeous scenery
Catch a football game at Auburn or Alabama for a true Southern cultural experience

states.name: Alaska
hiddenGems: Hatcher Pass — stunning alpine scenery and the historic Independence Mine
McCarthy and Kennicott — remote ghost town deep in Wrangell-St. Elias
Homer Spit — a narrow 4.5-mile strip of land jutting into Kachemak Bay
Kodiak Island — incredible wildlife viewing away from the cruise ship crowds
mustSeeSpots: Denali National Park — home to the highest peak in North America
Glacier Bay National Park — massive tidewater glaciers and whales
Kenai Fjords National Park — Exit Glacier and dramatic coastal fjords
Inside Passage — ferry or cruise through Southeast Alaska's stunning waterways
nationalParks: Gates of the Arctic National Park & Preserve
insiderTips: Book accommodations and tours months in advance — summer fills up fast
Take the Alaska Railroad from Anchorage to Denali for jaw-dropping views
The Alaska Marine Highway ferry system is an affordable alternative to cruises
Visit Fairbanks in winter for Northern Lights viewing from September to April

states.name: Arizona
hiddenGems: Chiricahua National Monument — stunning rock spires called the Wonderland of Rocks
Kartchner Caverns State Park — pristine living cave with incredible formations
Havasu Falls — turquoise waterfalls on the Havasupai Reservation (permit required)
Bisbee — quirky old mining town turned artist colony near the Mexican border
mustSeeSpots: Grand Canyon National Park — one of the seven natural wonders of the world
Sedona — red rock formations, vortex sites, and world-class hiking
Monument Valley — iconic Navajo Nation landscape of sandstone buttes
Antelope Canyon — stunning slot canyon near Page with light beams
insiderTips: The Grand Canyon North Rim has a fraction of the crowds and is equally stunning
Havasu Falls permits sell out in minutes — set alerts for the release date
Explore the Apache Trail scenic drive east of Phoenix for rugged desert beauty
Sedona is best visited on weekdays; weekends bring heavy traffic on the main roads

states.name: Arkansas
hiddenGems: Crater of Diamonds State Park — the only place in the US where you can dig for real diamonds
Thorncrown Chapel in Eureka Springs — stunning glass chapel nestled in the Ozarks
Blanchard Springs Caverns — one of the most beautiful cave systems in America
Devil's Den State Park — Lee Creek Valley with rugged Ozark terrain and caves
mustSeeSpots: Hot Springs National Park — the only national park in a city, with historic bathhouses
Buffalo National River — America's first national river, perfect for canoeing
Eureka Springs — charming Victorian resort town in the Ozark Mountains
Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art in Bentonville — world-class and free
insiderTips: The Ozark Highlands Trail is one of the best long-distance hikes in the South
Bentonville has become a foodie destination thanks to Walmart headquarters culture
Float the Buffalo River in spring when water levels are ideal
Visit Eureka Springs during the fall foliage season for the best colors

states.name: California
hiddenGems: Lava Beds National Monument — over 700 caves in volcanic terrain
Anza-Borrego Desert State Park — wildflower super blooms in spring
Glass Beach in Fort Bragg — beach covered in colorful sea glass
Salvation Mountain near Niland — a vibrant folk art hill in the desert
mustSeeSpots: Yosemite National Park — El Capitan, Half Dome, and Yosemite Falls
Pacific Coast Highway — iconic coastal drive from Big Sur to beyond
San Francisco — Golden Gate Bridge, Alcatraz, and cable cars
Joshua Tree National Park — surreal desert landscape of twisted trees and boulders
insiderTips: Visit Yosemite on weekdays or in shoulder season to avoid crushing crowds
Channel Islands National Park is California's best-kept secret — incredible snorkeling
Highway 395 along the Eastern Sierra is one of the most scenic drives in the state
Taco trucks and stands often serve better Mexican food than sit-down restaurants

states.name: Colorado
hiddenGems: Black Canyon of the Gunnison — a dramatic, sheer-walled gorge often overlooked
Great Sand Dunes National Park — tallest sand dunes in North America
Maroon Bells near Aspen — the most photographed peaks in Colorado
Bishop Castle near Rye — a massive hand-built stone castle open to the public for free
mustSeeSpots: Rocky Mountain National Park — Trail Ridge Road and alpine tundra
Mesa Verde National Park — ancient cliff dwellings of the Ancestral Puebloans
Garden of the Gods in Colorado Springs — dramatic red rock formations
Red Rocks Amphitheatre near Denver — iconic outdoor concert venue
nationalParks: Great Sand Dunes National Park & Preserve
Black Canyon of the Gunnison National Park
insiderTips: Drink lots of water — altitude sickness is real, even in Denver at 5,280 feet
Timed entry reservations are required for Rocky Mountain NP in summer
The Million Dollar Highway (US-550) is one of the most dramatic drives in America
Colorado's 14ers (peaks over 14,000 ft) are bucket-list hikes — start with easier ones

states.name: Connecticut
hiddenGems: Gillette Castle State Park — an eccentric medieval-style castle overlooking the Connecticut River
Devil's Hopyard State Park — Chapman Falls and mysterious pothole formations
Weir Farm National Historical Park — the only national park dedicated to American painting
Kent Falls State Park — a 250-foot cascade and covered bridge in the Litchfield Hills
mustSeeSpots: Mystic Seaport Museum — a living history maritime museum and charming village
Yale University in New Haven — stunning campus with free world-class art galleries
Mark Twain House in Hartford — where Twain wrote his greatest works
Litchfield Hills — rolling countryside, covered bridges, and quintessential New England towns
nationalParks: New England National Scenic Trail (shared with MA)
insiderTips: New Haven has arguably the best pizza in America — try Frank Pepe's, Sally's, or Modern
Fall foliage peaks in mid-October in Connecticut — Route 169 is especially scenic
Explore the Connecticut Wine Trail through the rolling hills of the eastern part of the state
The Essex Steam Train and Riverboat ride is a great family outing along the Connecticut River

states.name: Delaware
hiddenGems: Bombay Hook National Wildlife Refuge — incredible bird watching and fall migrations
Fort Delaware on Pea Patch Island — a Civil War fortress accessible only by ferry
Winterthur Museum — Henry Francis du Pont's estate with 175 rooms of decorative arts
The Enchanted Woods at Winterthur — a whimsical fairy-tale garden for families
mustSeeSpots: Rehoboth Beach — the nation's summer capital with a classic boardwalk
Hagley Museum and Library — the original du Pont powder works on the Brandywine
Historic New Castle — beautifully preserved colonial town on the Delaware River
Cape Henlopen State Park — where the Delaware Bay meets the Atlantic Ocean
nationalParks: Captain John Smith Chesapeake National Historic Trail
insiderTips: Delaware has no sales tax — stock up on shopping in Wilmington or at Tanger Outlets
Dewey Beach is the livelier nightlife alternative to family-friendly Rehoboth
Dogfish Head Brewery in Milton offers tours and tastings of their famous craft beers
The Lewes-Cape May ferry connects Delaware to New Jersey and is a scenic shortcut

states.name: Florida
hiddenGems: Dry Tortugas National Park — a remote island fort reachable only by boat or seaplane
Devil's Den Prehistoric Spring — an underground spring inside a dry cave near Williston
Cedar Key — a sleepy Old Florida fishing village on the Gulf Coast
Apalachicola — charming forgotten town famous for the best oysters in the world
mustSeeSpots: Everglades National Park — a vast subtropical wilderness of sawgrass and mangroves
Kennedy Space Center — watch rocket launches and tour NASA facilities
Key West — the southernmost point in the continental US with a free-spirited vibe
St. Augustine — the oldest continuously occupied European settlement in the US
insiderTips: The springs of North Florida (Ichetucknee, Rainbow, Ginnie) are magical for swimming and tubing
Visit theme parks on weekdays and use crowd calendar websites to avoid the worst days
Skip Miami Beach traffic and explore Wynwood Walls and Little Havana instead
Sanibel Island is one of the best shelling beaches in the world — go at low tide

states.name: Georgia
hiddenGems: Providence Canyon State Park — Georgia's Little Grand Canyon with colorful eroded bluffs
Cumberland Island — wild horses roam this pristine barrier island (ferry access only)
Dahlonega — the site of America's first gold rush, now a charming wine region
Okefenokee Swamp — 438,000 acres of blackwater swamp with gators and cypress trees
mustSeeSpots: Savannah Historic District — gorgeous squares, Spanish moss, and Southern charm
Martin Luther King Jr. National Historical Park in Atlanta
Georgia Aquarium in Atlanta — one of the largest aquariums in the world
Tybee Island — Savannah's laid-back beach with a historic lighthouse
nationalParks: Martin Luther King Jr. National Historical Park
insiderTips: Savannah allows open containers downtown — grab a to-go cup on River Street
The Georgia coast barrier islands are far less crowded than Florida beaches
Atlanta's BeltLine trail is a must-walk for food, art, and people watching
Try a meat-and-three restaurant for an authentic Southern comfort food experience

states.name: Hawaii
hiddenGems: Molokai — the most Hawaiian island, untouched by mass tourism
Papkolea Green Sand Beach on Big Island — one of only four green sand beaches in the world
Waimea Canyon on Kauai — the Grand Canyon of the Pacific
Makapu'u Tide Pools on Oahu — natural ocean pools with sea life
mustSeeSpots: Haleakala National Park on Maui — sunrise above the clouds at 10,000 feet
Hawaii Volcanoes National Park — active volcanism on the Big Island
Na Pali Coast on Kauai — towering sea cliffs accessible by boat, helicopter, or trail
Pearl Harbor National Memorial on Oahu — a solemn and powerful historical site
nationalParks: Pu'uhonua o Honaunau National Historical Park
insiderTips: Visit local plate lunch spots instead of tourist restaurants — try loco moco and poke bowls
Haleakala sunrise reservations fill up weeks in advance — book early
Respect the aloha spirit and Hawaiian sacred sites — stay on marked trails
Kauai and Big Island offer the most nature; Oahu and Maui are more developed

states.name: Idaho
hiddenGems: Craters of the Moon National Monument — surreal volcanic landscape resembling the moon
City of Rocks National Reserve — granite spires popular with rock climbers
Bruneau Dunes State Park — the tallest single-structured sand dune in North America
Route of the Hiawatha bike trail — 15 miles through tunnels and over trestles in the Bitterroots
mustSeeSpots: Sawtooth National Recreation Area — jagged peaks and pristine alpine lakes
Shoshone Falls near Twin Falls — the Niagara of the West at 212 feet
Sun Valley — America's first destination ski resort
Hells Canyon — the deepest river gorge in North America (deeper than the Grand Canyon)
nationalParks: Craters of the Moon National Monument & Preserve
insiderTips: Boise's Basque Block has the largest Basque community outside Europe — try the food
Stanley, Idaho is one of the coldest towns in the US but has incredible hot springs nearby
The Salmon River is called the River of No Return — world-class whitewater rafting
Idaho wines from the Snake River Valley AVA are an emerging hidden gem

states.name: Illinois
hiddenGems: Starved Rock State Park — stunning canyons and waterfalls along the Illinois River
Cahokia Mounds — the largest pre-Columbian settlement north of Mexico (UNESCO site)
Garden of the Gods in Shawnee National Forest — dramatic sandstone formations
Galena — a perfectly preserved 1800s Main Street and Ulysses S. Grant's home
mustSeeSpots: Chicago — world-class architecture, deep-dish pizza, and the Art Institute
Route 66 heritage sites — Illinois is where the Mother Road begins
Abraham Lincoln Presidential Library and Museum in Springfield
Millennium Park in Chicago — the Bean, Crown Fountain, and Lurie Garden
nationalParks: Lewis and Clark National Historic Trail
insiderTips: Chicago's neighborhoods each have distinct character — explore beyond the Loop
The Shawnee National Forest in southern Illinois feels like a different state entirely
Try Italian beef sandwiches — Chicago's underrated signature food (not just deep-dish)
Starved Rock is best visited after rain when the waterfalls are flowing strong

states.name: Indiana
hiddenGems: Indiana Dunes National Park — 15 miles of Lake Michigan shoreline with diverse ecosystems
West Baden Springs Hotel — an opulent early 1900s hotel with the world's first large free-span dome
Marengo Cave — a US National Landmark with spectacular dripstone formations
Nashville, Indiana — a charming Brown County artist colony surrounded by gorgeous hills
mustSeeSpots: Indianapolis Motor Speedway — home of the Indy 500, the greatest spectacle in racing
Indiana Dunes National Park — stunning beaches and trails on Lake Michigan
Children's Museum of Indianapolis — the world's largest children's museum
Brown County State Park — the Little Smokies with beautiful fall foliage
nationalParks: George Rogers Clark National Historical Park
insiderTips: Attend the Indy 500 at least once — 300,000+ fans create an unmatched atmosphere
The tenderloin sandwich is Indiana's signature food — try it breaded and pan-fried
Brown County fall foliage rivals New England but without the crowds
Columbus, Indiana is an unexpected architectural gem with buildings by world-famous architects

states.name: Iowa
hiddenGems: Maquoketa Caves State Park — Iowa's largest collection of caves, all explorable
Effigy Mounds National Monument — ancient Native American burial mounds shaped like animals
Pikes Peak State Park — not the Colorado one; stunning Mississippi River bluff views
The High Trestle Trail Bridge near Madrid — a dramatic half-mile art installation bridge
mustSeeSpots: Field of Dreams movie site in Dyersville — the iconic baseball diamond still stands
Iowa State Fair in Des Moines — one of the most famous state fairs in America
Bridges of Madison County in Winterset — the covered bridges that inspired the novel
Amana Colonies — seven German villages preserving communal heritage
insiderTips: RAGBRAI (the annual bike ride across Iowa) is a beloved summer tradition
Iowa's Loess Hills along the western border are a rare geological formation found only here and in China
Des Moines has a surprisingly vibrant food and craft beer scene downtown
The Devonian Fossil Gorge near Iowa City reveals 375-million-year-old ocean floor

states.name: Kansas
hiddenGems: Tallgrass Prairie National Preserve — one of the last remaining tallgrass prairies on Earth
Mushroom Rock State Park — unusual mushroom-shaped sandstone formations
Strataca (Kansas Underground Salt Museum) in Hutchinson — 650 feet below the surface
Monument Rocks — chalk pyramids rising from the prairie, Kansas's first National Natural Landmark
mustSeeSpots: Flint Hills — endless rolling grasslands that are stunningly beautiful at sunset
Dodge City — the legendary Wild West cattle town with Boot Hill Museum
Cosmosphere in Hutchinson — a world-class space museum rivaling the Smithsonian
Oz Museum in Wamego — dedicated to The Wizard of Oz
nationalParks: Brown v. Board of Education National Historical Park
insiderTips: Drive the Flint Hills Scenic Byway in late April when ranchers burn the prairie — it's surreal
Kansas sunsets over open prairie are some of the most spectacular in the country
Chase the storms — Kansas is prime tornado alley and storm chasing tours operate here
Kansas City BBQ is technically in both Kansas and Missouri — try both sides

states.name: Kentucky
hiddenGems: Red River Gorge — world-class rock climbing and stunning natural arches
Ale-8-One bottling company in Winchester — Kentucky's own ginger-citrus soft drink
Land Between the Lakes National Recreation Area — 170,000 acres between two major lakes
Berea — the folk arts capital of Kentucky with incredible Appalachian craftsmanship
mustSeeSpots: Mammoth Cave National Park — the world's longest known cave system (400+ miles)
Louisville — Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs, Louisville Slugger Museum, bourbon distilleries
The Kentucky Bourbon Trail — visit iconic distilleries like Maker's Mark and Woodford Reserve
Cumberland Falls — the Niagara of the South, famous for its rare moonbow
nationalParks: Abraham Lincoln Birthplace National Historical Park
Big South Fork National River & Recreation Area
insiderTips: Visit Cumberland Falls during a full moon to see the moonbow — one of only two in the Western Hemisphere
The bourbon trail is best done over multiple days — don't try to rush all distilleries
Kentucky Hot Brown is a must-try open-faced turkey sandwich invented at The Brown Hotel
Red River Gorge's Miguel's Pizza is a legendary climber hangout with great pizza and camping

states.name: Louisiana
hiddenGems: Poverty Point World Heritage Site — 3,400-year-old earthworks built by ancient Americans
Natchitoches — the oldest permanent settlement in the Louisiana Purchase territory
Atchafalaya Basin — the largest river swamp in the US, best explored by kayak or airboat
Abita Mystery House in Abita Springs — a wonderfully bizarre folk art museum
mustSeeSpots: New Orleans French Quarter — jazz, beignets, and Creole architecture
Cajun Country (Lafayette area) — Cajun music, food, and bayou culture
Plantation Road along the Great River Road — antebellum homes and difficult history
New Orleans City Park — a 1,300-acre oasis with ancient live oaks and a sculpture garden
nationalParks: Jean Lafitte National Historical Park & Preserve
Cane River Creole National Historical Park
New Orleans Jazz National Historical Park
insiderTips: Visit New Orleans outside of Mardi Gras for lower prices but still great music and food
Eat where locals eat — ask for recommendations in neighborhoods beyond the French Quarter
A swamp tour is a must — choose a small-boat tour for a more intimate experience
Boudin and cracklins from gas stations and meat markets in Cajun Country are incredible

states.name: Maine
hiddenGems: Baxter State Park — home to Mount Katahdin, the northern terminus of the Appalachian Trail
Monhegan Island — a remote artist colony with dramatic cliffs, reachable by ferry
Moxie Falls — Maine's tallest waterfall, a short hike near The Forks
Bold Coast trails in Cutler — rugged coastal hiking on the Downeast coast
mustSeeSpots: Acadia National Park — the crown jewel of the East Coast with mountains meeting the sea
Portland — one of America's best food cities with an incredible waterfront
Bass Harbor Head Lighthouse — an iconic lighthouse perched on Acadia's cliffs
Camden and Rockland — charming coastal towns and the schooner capital of Maine
nationalParks: Katahdin Woods and Waters National Monument
Saint Croix Island International Historic Site
insiderTips: Eat lobster at a lobster pound on the water, not at a tourist restaurant — much cheaper and fresher
Acadia's Park Loop Road is best experienced at sunrise with the Cadillac Mountain summit
The Schoodic Peninsula section of Acadia has almost no crowds compared to Bar Harbor
Maine blueberries in August are a revelation — pick your own at roadside farms

states.name: Maryland
hiddenGems: Calvert Cliffs State Park — hunt for Miocene-era shark teeth and fossils on the beach
Catoctin Mountain Park — the park that contains Camp David (though you can't visit it)
Smith Island — Maryland's only inhabited island in the Chesapeake Bay, famous for its cake
Chesapeake & Ohio Canal towpath — 184.5 miles of flat biking and hiking along the Potomac
mustSeeSpots: Annapolis — the sailing capital of the US and home to the Naval Academy
Baltimore Inner Harbor — National Aquarium, Fort McHenry, and Fells Point
Assateague Island — wild horses roam the beaches of this barrier island
Antietam National Battlefield — site of the bloodiest single day in American history
nationalParks: Chesapeake & Ohio Canal National Historical Park
Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad National Historical Park
insiderTips: Maryland blue crabs are best from May to September — Old Bay seasoning is essential
Skip the tourist crab spots and find a local crab house where you pick your own
The Eastern Shore is a world away from DC suburbs — slower pace and beautiful farmland
Deep Creek Lake in western Maryland is a great four-season mountain getaway

states.name: Massachusetts
hiddenGems: The Trustees' properties — lesser-known coastal preserves and historic estates across the state
Halibut Point State Park in Rockport — a former granite quarry with stunning ocean views
Mount Greylock — the highest point in Massachusetts with panoramic views of five states
Shelburne Falls — a charming village with a glacial pothole swimming hole and Bridge of Flowers
mustSeeSpots: Boston's Freedom Trail — 2.5 miles of revolutionary history through the city
Cape Cod National Seashore — 40 miles of pristine beaches and sand dunes
Salem — witch trials history, Peabody Essex Museum, and Halloween festivities
Martha's Vineyard and Nantucket — iconic New England island getaways
nationalParks: Boston Harbor Islands National Recreation Area
New Bedford Whaling National Historical Park
insiderTips: Walk the entire Freedom Trail rather than taking a tour — it's well-marked and self-guided
Visit Cape Cod in September when beaches are warm but summer crowds have left
Skip Faneuil Hall tourist food — head to the North End for authentic Italian
The Berkshires in western MA offer world-class performing arts at Tanglewood in summer

states.name: Michigan
hiddenGems: Pictured Rocks National Lakeshore — multicolored sandstone cliffs on Lake Superior
Tahquamenon Falls — the third-largest waterfall east of the Mississippi
Sleeping Bear Dunes — voted the most beautiful place in America by Good Morning America
Isle Royale National Park — the least visited national park in the lower 48, wild and remote
mustSeeSpots: Mackinac Island — no cars allowed on this charming Victorian island in Lake Huron
Sleeping Bear Dunes National Lakeshore — towering sand dunes overlooking Lake Michigan
Detroit — incredible comeback city with the Motown Museum, DIA, and great food
Traverse City — the cherry capital with gorgeous vineyards and beaches
insiderTips: The Upper Peninsula (UP) feels like a different state — visit for waterfalls, solitude, and pasties
Mackinac Island fudge shops are legendary — it's sometimes called Fudge Island
Michigan's west coast beaches rival Caribbean-clear water on warm summer days
Detroit-style pizza (thick, crispy, cheese-to-the-edge) is a must-try

states.name: Minnesota
hiddenGems: Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness — over a million acres of pristine lakes and portages
Pipestone National Monument — sacred quartzite quarries used by Native Americans for centuries
Soudan Underground Mine State Park — descend 2,341 feet into Minnesota's oldest iron mine
Lanesboro — a charming bluff country town with biking, tubing, and Amish culture
mustSeeSpots: Boundary Waters Canoe Area — world-class wilderness canoeing and camping
Minneapolis — vibrant arts scene, Mill City Museum, and the Chain of Lakes
North Shore Scenic Drive along Lake Superior — waterfalls, state parks, and Grand Marais
Voyageurs National Park — a water-based park accessible primarily by boat
nationalParks: Mississippi National River and Recreation Area
insiderTips: Boundary Waters permits are limited and competitive — reserve as early as possible
The North Shore of Lake Superior in fall is Minnesota's best-kept scenic secret
Minneapolis has more theater seats per capita than anywhere outside NYC
Try a Juicy Lucy (cheese-stuffed burger) at Matt's Bar or the 5-8 Club in Minneapolis

states.name: Mississippi
hiddenGems: Windsor Ruins — 23 haunting columns from a Civil War-era mansion in the countryside
Rowan Oak in Oxford — William Faulkner's home with his outline written on the wall
Red Bluff (Mississippi's Little Grand Canyon) — eroded red clay bluffs near Foxworth
Vicksburg National Military Park — one of the most well-preserved Civil War battlefields
mustSeeSpots: Natchez Trace Parkway — a 444-mile scenic drive following an ancient trail
Mississippi Delta — the birthplace of the blues, from Clarksdale to Indianola
Natchez — gorgeous antebellum mansions along the bluffs of the Mississippi River
Oxford — the charming university town home to Ole Miss, Faulkner, and great food
nationalParks: Brices Cross Roads National Battlefield Site
Medgar and Myrlie Evers Home National Monument
insiderTips: Visit Ground Zero Blues Club in Clarksdale for authentic Delta blues in a raw setting
The tamale trail through the Mississippi Delta serves unique Delta-style hot tamales
Gulf Islands National Seashore offers beautiful barrier island beaches near Biloxi
Oxford's Square has become one of the best food scenes in the South

states.name: Missouri
hiddenGems: Ha Ha Tonka State Park — castle ruins perched above a natural spring and karst landscape
Johnson's Shut-Ins State Park — natural water slides formed by billion-year-old rock
Meramec Caverns — legendary cave system along Route 66, once used as a hideout by Jesse James
Hermann — a charming German wine village along the Missouri River
mustSeeSpots: Gateway Arch National Park in St. Louis — the tallest man-made monument in the US
Kansas City BBQ — burnt ends, ribs, and legendary joints like Joe's KC and Q39
Ozark National Scenic Riverways — crystal-clear spring-fed rivers for floating
Branson — live entertainment capital of the world in the heart of the Ozarks
nationalParks: Harry S Truman National Historic Site
insiderTips: Kansas City and St. Louis have a fierce BBQ rivalry — try both to decide for yourself
Float the Current River or Jacks Fork for some of the clearest water in the Midwest
The Lake of the Ozarks is Missouri's summer playground with 1,150 miles of shoreline
City Museum in St. Louis is not a museum — it's a massive adult-sized playground and it's extraordinary

states.name: Montana
hiddenGems: Glacier National Park's Many Glacier area — fewer crowds than Going-to-the-Sun Road
Makoshika State Park near Glendive — badlands with dinosaur fossils
Beartooth Highway — called the most beautiful drive in America by Charles Kuralt
Garnet Ghost Town — one of Montana's best-preserved ghost towns
mustSeeSpots: Glacier National Park — Going-to-the-Sun Road and alpine lakes of staggering beauty
Yellowstone National Park (Montana entrance) — geysers, wildlife, and the Lamar Valley
Big Sky — world-class skiing with the biggest skiing in America
Little Bighorn Battlefield National Monument — site of Custer's Last Stand
insiderTips: Going-to-the-Sun Road opens late June or early July depending on snow — check before visiting
The Lamar Valley in Yellowstone is the Serengeti of North America for wildlife watching
Montana has no sales tax, making it a great place to shop for outdoor gear
Book Glacier NP accommodations a year in advance — they sell out extremely quickly

states.name: Nebraska
hiddenGems: Toadstool Geologic Park — surreal mushroom-shaped rock formations in the northwest
Sandhills — the largest sand dune formation in the Western Hemisphere, covered in grass
Carhenge near Alliance — a replica of Stonehenge made from vintage cars
Indian Cave State Park — petroglyphs, fall foliage, and Missouri River bluffs
mustSeeSpots: Chimney Rock National Historic Site — iconic landmark on the Oregon Trail
Sandhill Crane migration on the Platte River — 500,000 cranes each March
Omaha's Old Market — historic district with great restaurants and shops
Scotts Bluff National Monument — dramatic bluffs that guided pioneers westward
insiderTips: The sandhill crane migration in March is one of the greatest wildlife spectacles on Earth
Nebraska's Sandhills are eerily beautiful and incredibly remote — bring extra gas
Omaha has a world-class zoo — the Henry Doorly Zoo consistently ranks among the best
Husker football games in Lincoln are a cultural experience even for non-fans

states.name: Nevada
hiddenGems: Valley of Fire State Park — spectacular red sandstone formations just north of Las Vegas
International Car Forest of the Last Church near Goldfield — cars planted nose-down in the desert
Lamoille Canyon in the Ruby Mountains — the Swiss Alps of Nevada
Fly Geyser near Gerlach — an otherworldly man-made geyser on private land (tours available)
mustSeeSpots: Las Vegas Strip — love it or hate it, it's an experience everyone should have once
Red Rock Canyon National Conservation Area — stunning desert scenery minutes from Vegas
Great Basin National Park — Lehman Caves and bristlecone pines (the oldest trees on Earth)
Hoover Dam — an engineering marvel straddling the Nevada-Arizona border
nationalParks: Tule Springs Fossil Beds National Monument
insiderTips: Valley of Fire is a fraction of the cost and crowds of nearby national parks — don't skip it
Great Basin's dark skies make it one of the best stargazing spots in the country
Virginia City is a well-preserved Old West mining town worth the detour
Nevada's US-50 is dubbed the Loneliest Road in America — a bucket-list drive

states.name: New Hampshire
hiddenGems: Franconia Notch State Park — the Flume Gorge, a stunning natural granite chasm
Mount Washington Cog Railway — the world's first mountain-climbing cog railway
Lost River Gorge in Kinsman Notch — a network of caves, gorges, and boardwalks
Star Island — a remote island conference center off the coast, open for day visits
mustSeeSpots: White Mountains — Mount Washington, Presidential Range, and the Kancamagus Highway
Kancamagus Highway — one of the best fall foliage drives in America
Lake Winnipesaukee — a classic New England lake vacation destination
Portsmouth — a charming coastal city with colonial architecture and great restaurants
insiderTips: Mount Washington has the worst weather in the world outside polar regions — dress accordingly
The Kancamagus Highway in peak foliage (early October) is unforgettable but crowded on weekends
New Hampshire has no state sales or income tax — great for shopping
Hike the Franconia Ridge Loop for one of the best above-treeline ridge walks in the East

states.name: New Jersey
hiddenGems: Pine Barrens — a vast, wild forest covering 22% of New Jersey with unique ecology
Grounds for Sculpture in Hamilton — an incredible outdoor sculpture park and museum
High Point State Park — New Jersey's highest point with panoramic views of three states
Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park — a powerful 77-foot waterfall in a city
mustSeeSpots: Cape May — the country's oldest seaside resort with beautiful Victorian architecture
Jersey Shore boardwalks — Asbury Park, Point Pleasant, and Wildwood
Liberty State Park — stunning views of the Statue of Liberty and Ellis Island
Delaware Water Gap National Recreation Area — forested mountains and waterfalls
nationalParks: Delaware Water Gap National Recreation Area
Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park
insiderTips: New Jersey diner culture is legendary — most diners are open 24/7 with massive menus
The Pine Barrens are surprisingly wild and remote — great for kayaking and hiking
Taylor ham (or pork roll) egg and cheese on a hard roll is the quintessential NJ breakfast
Island Beach State Park offers pristine beaches without the boardwalk crowds

states.name: New Mexico
hiddenGems: Kasha-Katuwe Tent Rocks National Monument — otherworldly cone-shaped rock formations
Bisti/De-Na-Zin Wilderness — surreal badlands with hoodoos and petrified logs
Meow Wolf in Santa Fe — an immersive, mind-bending art experience inside a building
City of Rocks State Park — volcanic boulders forming a city of stone in the desert
mustSeeSpots: Carlsbad Caverns National Park — a massive underground chamber with spectacular formations
White Sands National Park — miles of pure white gypsum dunes
Santa Fe — the oldest state capital, with adobe architecture, art galleries, and green chile
Taos Pueblo — a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the oldest continually inhabited communities in the US
insiderTips: Always answer 'Christmas' when asked 'red or green?' — it means both chile sauces on your dish
Hatch chile season in late August/September is a culinary event — roasting chile is everywhere
The Albuquerque International Balloon Fiesta in October is one of the world's great spectacles
Santa Fe's Canyon Road has over 100 art galleries — one of the best art walks in America

states.name: New York
hiddenGems: Letchworth State Park — the Grand Canyon of the East with three major waterfalls on the Genesee River
Watkins Glen State Park — a stunning gorge with 19 waterfalls along a mile-long trail
The Catskills — mountains, waterfalls, and charming towns just 2 hours from NYC
Boldt Castle on Heart Island in the Thousand Islands — a fairy-tale castle on the St. Lawrence
mustSeeSpots: New York City — Central Park, Statue of Liberty, Times Square, Brooklyn Bridge, and world-class museums
Niagara Falls — the thundering power of one of the world's most famous waterfalls
Adirondack Park — the largest park in the contiguous US with 46 High Peaks
Hudson Valley — fall foliage, wineries, farm-to-table dining, and historic estates
nationalParks: Ellis Island National Museum of Immigration
Theodore Roosevelt Birthplace National Historic Site
Women's Rights National Historical Park in Seneca Falls
insiderTips: Upstate New York is a completely different world from NYC — lakes, mountains, and wine country
The Finger Lakes wine region produces outstanding Rieslings and is far less crowded than Napa
In NYC, skip Times Square restaurants and explore ethnic food in Queens or the Bronx
Take the Adirondack Railroad or drive through the Adirondacks in fall for incredible foliage

states.name: North Carolina
hiddenGems: Linville Gorge Wilderness — the Grand Canyon of the East with rugged backcountry hiking
Dry Falls near Highlands — walk behind a 65-foot waterfall without getting wet
Ocracoke Island — a remote Outer Banks village accessible only by ferry
Grandfather Mountain — the Mile High Swinging Bridge with panoramic views
mustSeeSpots: Blue Ridge Parkway — America's Favorite Drive winding through the Appalachians
Great Smoky Mountains National Park — the most visited national park in America
Outer Banks — Cape Hatteras, wild horses, and the Wright Brothers National Memorial
Asheville — craft beer capital, Biltmore Estate, and a vibrant arts scene
nationalParks: Carl Sandburg Home National Historic Site
insiderTips: Eastern NC BBQ (whole hog, vinegar-based) and Western/Lexington-style (tomato-based) are both must-tries
The Blue Ridge Parkway Milepost 300-400 stretch has the most dramatic mountain views
Asheville's South Slope neighborhood is brewery-crawl heaven
The Outer Banks are best in September/October — warm water, fewer crowds, great surf

states.name: North Dakota
hiddenGems: Enchanted Highway — a 32-mile stretch of massive metal sculptures along a rural road
Maah Daah Hey Trail — a 144-mile world-class singletrack mountain biking trail
International Peace Garden — a botanical garden straddling the US-Canada border
Knife River Indian Villages — earth lodge reconstructions of Hidatsa villages
mustSeeSpots: Theodore Roosevelt National Park — the badlands that inspired a president to conserve
Medora — a charming frontier town at the gateway to the national park
Fargo — surprisingly vibrant downtown with great food and craft beer
Lewis and Clark Interpretive Center in Washburn — near the expedition's winter camp
nationalParks: Theodore Roosevelt National Park (North and South Units)
Knife River Indian Villages National Historic Site
Fort Union Trading Post National Historic Site
insiderTips: Theodore Roosevelt NP is severely underrated — bison, wild horses, and stunning badlands with few crowds
The Medora Musical is a beloved nightly outdoor show in summer
Drive between the North and South Units of TR National Park for big sky prairie scenery
Knoephla soup (a German-Russian dumpling soup) is North Dakota's signature comfort food

states.name: Ohio
hiddenGems: Hocking Hills State Park — Old Man's Cave, Ash Cave, and Cedar Falls in stunning gorges
Serpent Mound — the largest effigy mound in the world, shaped like an uncoiling serpent
Cuyahoga Valley National Park — waterfalls and the scenic railroad between Cleveland and Akron
Yellow Springs — a quirky progressive village with Glen Helen Nature Preserve
mustSeeSpots: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in Cleveland — the history of American music
Cuyahoga Valley National Park — Ohio's only national park with Brandywine Falls
Amish Country in Holmes County — rolling farms, handmade goods, and incredible food
Cincinnati — Over-the-Rhine historic neighborhood, Cincinnati chili, and great parks
nationalParks: Perry's Victory and International Peace Memorial
James A. Garfield National Historic Site
Charles Young Buffalo Soldiers National Monument
insiderTips: Hocking Hills is best visited on weekdays — weekends are extremely crowded
Cincinnati chili is its own thing (served over spaghetti) — approach it with an open mind
The Lake Erie Islands (Put-in-Bay, Kelleys Island) are Ohio's summer party and nature destinations
Columbus has quietly become one of the best food cities in the Midwest

states.name: Oklahoma
hiddenGems: Wichita Mountains Wildlife Refuge — bison, longhorn cattle, and granite peaks in the prairie
Turner Falls Park — Oklahoma's largest waterfall with a natural swimming pool
Tallgrass Prairie Preserve — the largest protected remnant of tallgrass prairie on Earth
The Blue Whale of Catoosa — a quirky Route 66 roadside attraction
mustSeeSpots: Oklahoma City National Memorial — a powerful tribute to the 1995 bombing
Route 66 through Oklahoma — the state has more drivable miles of the Mother Road than any other
Philbrook Museum of Art in Tulsa — world-class art in a stunning Italian Renaissance villa
Chickasaw National Recreation Area — freshwater and mineral springs in the Arbuckle Mountains
insiderTips: Oklahoma has 39 tribal nations — explore Native American cultural centers and powwows
Tulsa's Gathering Place is one of the best public parks in America — and it's free
Oklahoma onion burgers are a regional delicacy — try them at Sid's Diner in El Reno
The Ouachita National Forest in southeastern Oklahoma has surprisingly lush mountain scenery

states.name: Oregon
hiddenGems: Alvord Desert — a vast dry lakebed in southeastern Oregon that feels like another planet
Samuel H. Boardman Scenic Corridor — the most dramatic stretch of the Oregon Coast
Painted Hills — one of Oregon's Seven Wonders with colorful striped hills
Steens Mountain — a massive fault-block mountain rising a mile above the Alvord Desert
mustSeeSpots: Crater Lake National Park — the deepest lake in the US with impossibly blue water
Columbia River Gorge — Multnomah Falls and dozens of other waterfalls
Oregon Coast — Cape Perpetua, Cannon Beach's Haystack Rock, and Thor's Well
Portland — food carts, Powell's Books, and craft beer culture
nationalParks: John Day Fossil Beds National Monument
Lewis and Clark National Historical Park
insiderTips: Oregon has no sales tax — great for shopping
The Oregon Coast is beautiful year-round but winter storms bring dramatic wave watching
Smith Rock State Park near Bend is the birthplace of American sport climbing
Central Oregon (Bend area) has 300 days of sunshine compared to rainy Portland

states.name: Pennsylvania
hiddenGems: Ricketts Glen State Park — 21 named waterfalls along a single hiking trail
Fallingwater — Frank Lloyd Wright's masterpiece built over a waterfall in the Laurel Highlands
Pine Creek Gorge (Pennsylvania Grand Canyon) — 47 miles of dramatic gorge scenery
Centralia — a ghost town above an underground coal fire that's been burning since 1962
mustSeeSpots: Philadelphia — Independence Hall, Liberty Bell, and the best cheesesteaks
Gettysburg National Military Park — the turning point of the Civil War
Pittsburgh — a reinvented steel city with great food, bridges, and The Andy Warhol Museum
Pennsylvania Dutch Country — Amish farms, covered bridges, and shoofly pie
nationalParks: Delaware Water Gap National Recreation Area (shared with NJ)
insiderTips: The Philly cheesesteak debate (Pat's vs. Geno's) is for tourists — locals have their own favorites
The Laurel Highlands have some of the best outdoor recreation in the East
Driving through Lancaster County on back roads reveals stunning Amish farmland
Pittsburgh's incline railways offer the best views of the city's dramatic three-river confluence

states.name: Rhode Island
hiddenGems: Block Island — a car-free-friendly island with stunning Mohegan Bluffs and Southeast Light
Beavertail State Park in Jamestown — dramatic rocky coastline and one of America's oldest lighthouses
Federal Hill in Providence — an incredible Italian neighborhood with outstanding restaurants
Cliff Walk in Newport — 3.5 miles of oceanside trail past Gilded Age mansions
mustSeeSpots: Newport Mansions — The Breakers, Marble House, and other Gilded Age palaces
Providence — a revitalized creative city with WaterFire, RISD Museum, and great dining
Block Island — a charming New England island with beaches and bluffs
Narragansett Bay — sailing, beaches, and the quintessential Rhode Island coast
nationalParks: Blackstone River Valley National Historical Park
insiderTips: Rhode Island is tiny — you can drive across the whole state in under an hour
Del's Frozen Lemonade and coffee milk are quintessential Rhode Island treats
Newport's International Tennis Hall of Fame is worth a visit even for non-tennis fans
WaterFire in Providence (bonfires lit on the river) happens select evenings May through November

states.name: South Carolina
hiddenGems: Congaree National Park — the largest intact expanse of old-growth bottomland forest in the US
Hunting Island State Park — a semi-tropical barrier island with an accessible lighthouse
Falls Park on the Reedy in Greenville — a stunning urban waterfall park
Daufuskie Island — a Gullah heritage island accessible only by boat
mustSeeSpots: Charleston — one of America's most charming cities with historic architecture and incredible food
Myrtle Beach — the classic South Carolina beach vacation destination
Beaufort and the Lowcountry — Spanish moss, Gullah culture, and barrier islands
Greenville — a revitalized downtown with falls, a swinging bridge, and great restaurants
nationalParks: Fort Sumter and Fort Moultrie National Historical Park
insiderTips: Charleston's food scene is world-class — Husk, FIG, and countless incredible restaurants
Lowcountry boils (shrimp, corn, sausage, potatoes) are a quintessential local experience
Edisto Island is a quieter, less commercial alternative to Myrtle Beach
Sullivan's Island near Charleston has beautiful beaches without the tourist-trap feel

states.name: South Dakota
hiddenGems: Wind Cave National Park — one of the longest caves in the world with rare boxwork formations
Custer State Park — the best wildlife loop drive in the US with bison, pronghorn, and burros
Spearfish Canyon Scenic Byway — waterfalls, limestone cliffs, and gorgeous fall colors
Minuteman Missile National Historic Site — a Cold War-era missile silo open for tours
mustSeeSpots: Mount Rushmore National Memorial — the iconic presidential sculpture in the Black Hills
Badlands National Park — dramatic eroded buttes and the richest fossil beds in the world
Crazy Horse Memorial — the world's largest mountain carving in progress
Deadwood — the legendary Wild West town, now a National Historic Landmark
insiderTips: Visit the Badlands at sunrise or sunset when the colors are most dramatic
Custer State Park's Wildlife Loop is best done early morning — bison often block the road
Wall Drug in Wall is a tourist trap but a fun one — and the water is free
The Black Hills have way more to offer than just Mount Rushmore — plan multiple days

states.name: Tennessee
hiddenGems: Fall Creek Falls State Park — the tallest waterfall in the eastern US at 256 feet
Lynchburg — home of Jack Daniel's Distillery in a charming dry-county town
Rugby — a restored Victorian English village in the Cumberland Plateau
Reelfoot Lake — a natural lake created by the 1811 earthquake, famous for bald eagle watching
mustSeeSpots: Great Smoky Mountains National Park — the most visited national park in America
Nashville — live music on Broadway, the Grand Ole Opry, and hot chicken
Memphis — Graceland, Beale Street, and the National Civil Rights Museum at the Lorraine Motel
Dollywood in Pigeon Forge — Dolly Parton's beloved Smoky Mountain theme park
nationalParks: Big South Fork National River & Recreation Area
insiderTips: Nashville hot chicken is a must — start at Prince's, the original, then explore others
The Smokies are most crowded in October — visit in spring for wildflowers with fewer people
Memphis BBQ (dry-rubbed ribs) is legendary — Central BBQ and Payne's are local favorites
Cades Cove in the Smokies is best visited before 9am to avoid traffic jams

states.name: Texas
hiddenGems: Palo Duro Canyon — the second-largest canyon in the US, often called the Grand Canyon of Texas
Marfa — a tiny West Texas art town with mysterious lights and minimalist installations
Enchanted Rock — a massive pink granite dome sacred to Native Americans near Fredericksburg
Hamilton Pool Preserve — a stunning natural grotto pool near Austin
mustSeeSpots: Big Bend National Park — dramatic desert wilderness along the Rio Grande
San Antonio River Walk — a charming below-street-level walkway along the river
The Alamo — the most famous site in Texas, in the heart of San Antonio
Austin — the live music capital of the world with 6th Street and incredible BBQ
nationalParks: San Antonio Missions National Historical Park
insiderTips: Texas BBQ is a religion — Franklin Barbecue in Austin, Snow's in Lexington, Goldee's in Fort Worth
Big Bend is extremely remote — fill up on gas and bring supplies before entering
Breakfast tacos are a daily ritual in Texas — find a local taqueria for the real deal
The Texas Hill Country (Fredericksburg, Wimberley, Dripping Springs) is beautiful in spring with wildflowers

states.name: Utah
hiddenGems: Goblin Valley State Park — thousands of mushroom-shaped sandstone formations
Bonneville Salt Flats — a surreal white expanse perfect for land speed records and photography
Escalante Petrified Forest State Park — petrified wood and a reservoir in red rock country
Dead Horse Point State Park — views that rival the Grand Canyon overlooking the Colorado River
mustSeeSpots: The Mighty Five National Parks — Zion, Bryce Canyon, Capitol Reef, Canyonlands, Arches
Zion National Park — the Narrows and Angels Landing are bucket-list hikes
Monument Valley — iconic buttes on the Utah-Arizona border (Navajo Nation)
Park City — world-class skiing and the Sundance Film Festival
insiderTips: Capitol Reef is the least visited of the Mighty Five and has free fruit picking in the Fruita orchards
Angels Landing now requires a permit — enter the lottery in advance
Utah's scenic byways (Highway 12, Highway 24) are as stunning as the national parks themselves
Visit Arches at dawn or dusk — midday crowds and heat can be overwhelming

states.name: Vermont
hiddenGems: Stowe Pinnacle Trail — a moderate hike with panoramic views of the Green Mountains
Quechee Gorge — Vermont's Little Grand Canyon, 165 feet deep
Rock of Ages granite quarry in Barre — the world's largest deep-hole granite quarry
Kingdom Trails in East Burke — one of the best mountain biking trail networks in the world
mustSeeSpots: Stowe — a picturesque ski town with covered bridges and mountain scenery
Ben & Jerry's Factory in Waterbury — take the factory tour and visit the Flavor Graveyard
Woodstock — one of the prettiest small towns in America with a covered bridge and Billings Farm
Green Mountain National Forest — hiking, camping, and the Long Trail (America's oldest long-distance trail)
insiderTips: Fall foliage in Vermont peaks late September to mid-October — Route 100 is the classic drive
Vermont has more covered bridges per square mile than any other state
Visit a sugarhouse during maple season (March-April) to see syrup being made and taste it fresh
Vermont's smallest state capital (Montpelier) has no McDonald's or chain fast food

states.name: Virginia
hiddenGems: Natural Bridge State Park — a 215-foot natural stone arch once owned by Thomas Jefferson
Chincoteague Island — wild ponies, pristine beaches, and a charming fishing village
Luray Caverns — the largest caverns in the eastern US with a stalactite organ
Virginia Creeper Trail — a 34-mile rail trail descending through gorgeous mountain scenery
mustSeeSpots: Shenandoah National Park — Skyline Drive, fall foliage, and Appalachian Trail access
Colonial Williamsburg — a living-history museum of colonial America
Arlington National Cemetery — a solemn tribute to America's fallen
Monticello and the University of Virginia — Thomas Jefferson's architectural legacy
nationalParks: Arlington House, The Robert E. Lee Memorial
Appomattox Court House National Historical Park
Wolf Trap National Park for the Performing Arts
insiderTips: Skyline Drive and the Blue Ridge Parkway connect for one continuous mountain drive
Virginia's wine country (Charlottesville, Loudoun County) has over 300 wineries
Virginia Beach has a great boardwalk, but the Eastern Shore is where you find solitude
Smith Mountain Lake is Virginia's best-kept secret for lakeside vacationing

states.name: Washington
hiddenGems: Palouse Falls — a dramatic 198-foot waterfall in the desert of eastern Washington
North Cascades National Park — the least visited national park in the lower 48 (besides Isle Royale)
San Juan Islands — orcas, kayaking, and charming island towns reachable by ferry
Leavenworth — a Bavarian-themed village nestled in the Cascades
mustSeeSpots: Olympic National Park — rainforest, mountains, and rugged Pacific coastline in one park
Mount Rainier National Park — the iconic volcanic peak with wildflower meadows
Seattle — Pike Place Market, Space Needle, and world-class coffee culture
North Cascades — jagged peaks, turquoise lakes, and over 300 glaciers
nationalParks: San Juan Island National Historical Park
Mount St. Helens National Volcanic Monument
insiderTips: Olympic National Park has three distinct ecosystems — budget at least 2-3 days
The San Juan Islands are best visited by taking the Washington State Ferry from Anacortes
Eastern Washington's wine regions (Walla Walla, Yakima) produce world-class wines
Hike the Hoh Rain Forest's Hall of Mosses trail — it feels like Jurassic Park

states.name: West Virginia
hiddenGems: New River Gorge National Park — America's newest national park with incredible bridge views and rafting
Seneca Rocks — dramatic 900-foot rock formation popular with climbers
Cass Scenic Railroad State Park — ride a steam locomotive up the mountain
Blackwater Falls State Park — a stunning 57-foot waterfall and canyon
mustSeeSpots: New River Gorge National Park & Preserve — whitewater rafting, hiking, and the iconic bridge
Harpers Ferry National Historical Park — where the Potomac and Shenandoah rivers meet
Dolly Sods Wilderness — a highland bog that feels more like Canada than Appalachia
Greenbrier Resort in White Sulphur Springs — a historic luxury resort with a secret Cold War bunker
nationalParks: New River Gorge National Park & Preserve
insiderTips: Bridge Day in October is the only day you can legally BASE jump off the New River Gorge Bridge
The Gauley River in fall is considered some of the best whitewater rafting in the world
West Virginia's back roads through the mountains are some of the best motorcycle routes in the East
Almost Heaven is the state's tourism slogan — and the mountain scenery truly earns it

states.name: Wisconsin
hiddenGems: Apostle Islands National Lakeshore — sea caves, lighthouses, and pristine islands on Lake Superior
Devil's Lake State Park — dramatic quartzite bluffs surrounding a pristine glacial lake
Cave of the Mounds — a national natural landmark near Blue Mounds
Stockholm and the Great River Road — charming small towns along the Mississippi
mustSeeSpots: Apostle Islands — sea kayaking through ice caves and visiting historic lighthouses
Door County — the Cape Cod of the Midwest with cherry orchards, fish boils, and lighthouses
Madison — a vibrant college town on an isthmus between two lakes
Milwaukee — Harley-Davidson Museum, craft beer, and a revitalized lakefront
insiderTips: A Door County fish boil is a must — the dramatic boilover is part of the show
Wisconsin supper clubs are a unique dining tradition — go for the Friday night fish fry
The Apostle Islands ice caves are only accessible in winter when the lake freezes — check conditions
Wisconsin cheese curds should squeak when you bite them — that means they're fresh

states.name: Wyoming
hiddenGems: Medicine Wheel National Historic Landmark — a sacred Native American stone circle at 10,000 feet
Thermopolis Hot Springs — the world's largest mineral hot spring, and the state bathhouse is free
Wind River Range — remote and rugged mountains with the best backcountry in Wyoming
Bighorn Canyon National Recreation Area — dramatic canyon and wild horses
mustSeeSpots: Yellowstone National Park — Old Faithful, Grand Prismatic Spring, and abundant wildlife
Grand Teton National Park — the most dramatic mountain skyline in the US
Devils Tower National Monument — America's first national monument, a striking volcanic neck
Jackson Hole — a world-class ski and outdoor recreation town at the base of the Tetons
insiderTips: Yellowstone is best experienced by entering from less popular entrances (East or Northeast)
The Lamar Valley at dawn is the best wildlife watching in the entire national park system
Wyoming is the least populated state — you can find genuine solitude here
Visit Grand Teton before Yellowstone — many people rush past the Tetons and miss the best views
